input.description: Read the prompt to understand the task

content.content:
The Elasticsearch cluster is running below the target version.

Your task:
Perform a full-restart upgrade to version 8.11.1 while keeping data intact.

Constraints:
- Do not install operators or CRDs.
- The cluster must be fully stopped and started again for the upgrade.

Verification:
1. Elasticsearch reports version 8.11.1.
2. Cluster is reachable and has 3 nodes.
3. Seeded data is still present.

content.thinking:
I can see:
1. The StatefulSet is using image `docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.0`
2. The updateStrategy is `OnDelete` - this means pods won't be automatically updated when the StatefulSet is changed
3. The task requires a full-restart upgrade (stop all, then start again)
4. The data is in PersistentVolumeClaims which will be preserved across pod restarts

The plan for a full-restart upgrade:
1. Check current data exists
2. Update the StatefulSet image to 8.11.1
3. Scale down to 0 to stop all pods completely
4. Scale back up to 3 to restart them with the new image
5. Wait for all pods to be ready and verify the upgrade succeeded

Since the updateStrategy is `OnDelete`, I'll update the image first, then scale down and back up to trigger the restart with the new version.

input.command: export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/examples-rererun/elasticsearch-full-restart-upgrade-ha-20260623_124727/bundle/kubeconfig && kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -s http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/ | python3 -m json.tool
input.description: Check current Elasticsearch version and cluster health

input.command: export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/examples-rererun/elasticsearch-full-restart-upgrade-ha-20260623_124727/bundle/kubeconfig && kubectl -n elasticsearch exec curl-test -- curl -s http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/app-data/_count

content.thinking:
Good. I can see:
- Current version: 8.11.0
- Need to upgrade to: 8.11.1
- Seeded data: 3 documents in `app-data` index - this is what needs to be preserved

The upgrade strategy:
1. Update the StatefulSet image to 8.11.1
2. Scale down to 0 (full stop)
3. Scale back up to 3 (full restart with new image)
4. Wait for all pods to be ready
5. Verify

Since updateStrategy is `OnDelete`, I need to:
1. Patch the StatefulSet with the new image
2. Scale to 0 to delete the existing pods
3. Scale back to 3 to spin up new pods with the updated image

The PVCs will stick around when I scale down because the persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy is set to `Retain`, so my seeded data stays intact.

content.text:
Current state confirmed: version 8.11.0, 3 seeded documents in `app-data`. Now I'll perform the full-restart upgrade.

**Step 1: Update the StatefulSet image to 8.11.1**

input.command: export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/examples-rererun/elasticsearch-full-restart-upgrade-ha-20260623_124727/bundle/kubeconfig && kubectl -n elasticsearch patch statefulset es-cluster --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/image", "value": "docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1"}]'

export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/examples-rererun/elasticsearch-full-restart-upgrade-ha-20260623_124727/bundle/kubeconfig && kubectl -n elasticsearch scale statefulset es-cluster --replicas=0 && echo "Scaled down to 0"

export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/examples-rererun/elasticsearch-full-restart-upgrade-ha-20260623_124727/bundle/kubeconfig && kubectl -n elasticsearch wait --for=delete pod/es-cluster-0 pod/es-cluster-1 pod/es-cluster-2 --timeout=120s && echo "All pods terminated"

content.text: All 3 ES nodes are fully stopped. **Step 3: Scale back up to 3 with the new image.**

input.command: export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/examples-rererun/elasticsearch-full-restart-upgrade-ha-20260623_124727/bundle/kubeconfig && kubectl -n elasticsearch scale statefulset es-cluster --replicas=3 && echo "Scaled up to 3"

export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/examples-rererun/elasticsearch-full-restart-upgrade-ha-20260623_124727/bundle/kubeconfig && kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -w &
PID=$!
sleep 5
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/examples-rererun/elasticsearch-full-restart-upgrade-ha-20260623_124727/bundle/kubeconfig
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  READY=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=es-cluster --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c "Running")
  echo "Attempt $i: $READY/3 pods running"
  if [ "$READY" -eq 3 ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods are running!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
kill $PID 2>/dev/null || true
